summary: Preserve the Tauri invoke receiver and avoid WebDriver error-envelope collisions in the cache probe.

decisions.decision: Call the page-owned Tauri invoke function with its owning internals object as receiver, and name retained probe failures diagnosticError so WebDriver does not mistake a successful diagnostic value for a remote protocol error envelope.
decisions.rationale: The first diagnostic Sandbox run at df6db80 stopped at the before-close snapshot with render-cache-list-rejected because the probe detached invoke from its owner. Returning an error string then caused the WebDriver client to promote the diagnostic object to a remote error. Host cleanup passed and no product cache conclusion was possible from that run.

testEvidence.criterion: The cache snapshot must preserve the Tauri invoke receiver and retain a non-protocol diagnostic field.
testEvidence.test: Run the complete focused M4 harness suite with a fake invoke function that records its receiver, followed by TypeScript no-emit.

red.outcome: RED: the bounded probe stopped before restart with render-cache-list-rejected; exact Sandbox cleanup passed.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/m4-packaged-walkthrough.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd run typecheck
green.outcome: GREEN: 22/22 harness tests passed, the invoke receiver was the Tauri internals object, and TypeScript no-emit passed.

nearMisses.confirmation: The added diagnostic itself rejected at the before-close boundary; no product cache record result was inferred, and host cleanup passed.

questions: No owner decision is required; this repairs the diagnostic boundary exposed by its first exact execution.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
